queue.name: ID10257: Theorem 2: Tidal Deformation Amplitude: ε

master_row.title: Theorem 2: Tidal Deformation Amplitude: ε
master_row.canonical_statement: The tidal deformation parameter is \[ \varepsilon = \left(\frac{M_{\text{Moon}}}{M_\oplus}\right) \left(\frac{R_\oplus}{d}\right)^{3} (1 + k_{2}), \] where $k_{2}$ is the Love number. Substituting values yields \[ \varepsilon \approx 7.27\times 10^{-8}, \] predicting a solid Earth tidal bulge \[ h = \varepsilon R_\oplus \approx 0.46~\mathrm{m}, \] matching seismological measurements. Ocean tides (meters) arise from resonant amplification in shallow seas, as predicted by wave equation solutions on realistic basins.
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Theorem 2: Tidal Deformation Amplitude’.
master_row.dictionary: The tidal deformation parameter is where k_2 is the Love number (ε)
master_row.encyclopedia: Theorem 2: Tidal Deformation Amplitude: ε is an algebraic definition, written ε = (fracM_MoonM_oplus ) ((R_oplus)/(d) )^3 (1 + k_2). It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from M_, M, R, k_2, defining ε.
